# 🔍 TrueTextAI

**Instantly detect whether text was written by a human or generated by AI.**

TrueTextAI is a machine learning-powered web application built with Streamlit. It takes any piece of text — pasted directly or uploaded as a `.txt` file — and classifies it as either **Human Written** or **AI Generated**, along with a probability breakdown and a confidence rating.

---

## ✨ Features

- **Dual input modes** — paste text directly or upload a `.txt` file
- **Instant classification** — Human vs. AI verdict with a single click
- **Probability scores** — percentage likelihood for both Human and AI labels
- **Confidence rating** — Very High / High / Moderate / Low based on prediction margin
- **Session history** — keeps track of the last 15 analyses within a session
- **Clean, polished UI** — custom-themed Streamlit interface with Space Grotesk typography

---

## 🗂️ Project Structure

```
TrueTextAI/
├── data/                    # Training data
├── TrueTextAI.ipynb         # Model training notebook
├── app.py                   # Streamlit web application
├── requirements.txt         # Python dependencies
└── truetextai_model.pkl     # Trained model (generated by the notebook)
```

---

## 🚀 Getting Started

### 1. Clone the repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Ritwika-dey/TrueTextAI.git
cd TrueTextAI
```

### 2. Install dependencies

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install streamlit
```

### 3. Train the model

Open and run all cells in `TrueTextAI.ipynb`. This will produce `truetextai_model.pkl` in the project root.

```bash
jupyter notebook TrueTextAI.ipynb
```

### 4. Launch the app

```bash
streamlit run app.py
```

The app will open at `http://localhost:8501`.

> ⚠️ The app requires `truetextai_model.pkl` to be present. Run the notebook first if the file is missing.

---

## 🧠 How It Works

The model uses a **scikit-learn classifier** trained on labeled human and AI-generated text samples. At inference time:

1. The input text is passed to the trained model.
2. `decision_function` produces a raw score, which is converted to a probability via sigmoid.
3. The app displays the Human %, AI %, and a confidence tier based on the winning probability.

---

## 🛠️ Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Web UI | Streamlit |
| ML / Modeling | scikit-learn, joblib |
| NLP / Features | NLTK, spaCy |
| Data & Analysis | NumPy, pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, SHAP |

---

## 📋 Requirements

```
numpy
pandas
matplotlib
seaborn
nltk
spacy
scikit-learn
shap
joblib
streamlit
```
